N1ED.description: N1ED is an editor for Bootstrap websites that enables block-by-block content building.
N1ED.history: N1ED is a WYSIWYG/block editor platform developed by EdSDK (Dmitry Komarov). It integrates as a plugin for CKEditor 4, TinyMCE, and standalone use, providing a visual content builder with file manager and image editor capabilities. Compatible with Drupal, Joomla, WordPress, Magento, and React. No major ownership changes documented.
N1ED.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal. N1ED is a niche web editor plugin with no consumer-facing surface or hosting capabilities that could facilitate abuse.

Nabble.description: Nabble is a platform offering free forum hosting and online web applications with customizable features.
Nabble.history: Nabble launched around 2006 as a free forum-hosting service that also let users embed discussion boards into websites. The platform gained modest traction in early web 2.0 era but largely stagnated as social media and modern forum platforms took over. It remains operational but shows little active development.
Nabble.summary_abuse: Nabble offers free hosted forum creation with minimal verification requirements, which has made it a minor vector for spam threads and low-effort scam posts. The platform's low activity and limited moderation infrastructure mean takedowns can be slow. Abuse volume is low given the platform's small size but the free anonymous hosting model is a risk factor.

Nacelle.description: Nacelle is a headless ecommerce platform.
Nacelle.history: Nacelle was founded in 2019 and raised venture funding to build a headless commerce platform focused on decoupling the frontend from backend ecommerce systems like Shopify and Magento. The company targets mid-market and enterprise merchants seeking performance improvements via composable commerce architecture.
Nacelle.summary_abuse: Nacelle is a B2B headless ecommerce infrastructure platform with no direct consumer-facing surface. It has no notable abuse signal.

NACEX.description: NACEX is an express courier company in Spain, Andorra and Portugal.
NACEX.history: NACEX was founded on February 6, 1995 in Barcelona, Spain, as an express courier company serving Spain, Portugal, and Andorra. In 2002 it was integrated into Grupo Logista, one of Europe's leading proximity distribution groups, which provided financial and technological support for its expansion. It operates primarily as a B2B courier and parcel delivery network for e-commerce and logistics.
NACEX.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal. NACEX is a physical parcel delivery and courier infrastructure service. It is occasionally used for fake delivery notification phishing scams impersonating its brand (common across all courier companies), but the company itself is a legitimate logistics provider with no known abuse of its systems.

NagaCommerce.description: NagaCommerce is an ecommerce platform provided as a service.
NagaCommerce.history: NagaCommerce is a Greek ecommerce SaaS platform developed by DiSPLAYiDEAS, launched around 2018-2019. It targets Greek online retailers and has not had any notable acquisitions or ownership changes.
NagaCommerce.summary_abuse: NagaCommerce has no notable abuse signal. It is a small regional ecommerce SaaS platform serving Greek businesses, with no documented use in phishing or fraud infrastructure.

Nagich.description: Nagich is a website accessibility overlay provider from Israel.
Nagich.history: Nagich is an Israeli accessibility overlay company offering AI-driven website accessibility solutions. It operates primarily in the Israeli market and targets websites needing WCAG compliance.
Nagich.summary_abuse: Nagich is a website accessibility overlay SaaS from Israel. Accessibility overlays as a category have been criticized by disability advocacy groups for inadequate compliance, but Nagich has no documented abuse signal in the fraud or phishing sense.

NagishLi.description: NagishLi is a free accessibility plugin from Localize*, created to provide an equal oppurtunity for webmasters to make their website accessible and make the internet more accessible for people with disability.
NagishLi.history: NagishLi is a free web accessibility plugin first released in 2016 by an Israeli team, designed to help webmasters meet accessibility standards for users with disabilities. No major ownership changes are recorded.
NagishLi.summary_abuse: NagishLi has no notable abuse signal. It is a website accessibility widget with no content-hosting or user-generated content features that would attract fraudsters.

Naive UI.description: Naive UI is a Vue.js UI library written in TypeScript, offering more than 80 treeshakable components.
Naive UI.history: Naive UI is an open-source Vue 3 UI component library created by TuSen AI (a Chinese autonomous vehicle company), first released around 2021. It is written entirely in TypeScript and offers 80+ treeshakable components. The project gained popularity in the Vue 3 ecosystem quickly.
Naive UI.summary_abuse: Naive UI is a client-side Vue.js component library with no server-side component. It has no notable abuse signal.

Najva.description: Najva is a retention marketing solution that offers push notification and email marketing.
Najva.history: Najva is an Iranian marketing SaaS company founded around 2016 that provides push notification and email marketing services, primarily serving the Persian-speaking market.
Najva.summary_abuse: Najva is a push notification and email marketing platform serving mainly the Iranian market. As a mass-communication tool it carries typical abuse risk for spam campaigns, though no specific large-scale documented abuse incidents are known. Its geographic focus on Iran limits its global abuse footprint.

Namastay.description: Namastay is an optimized booking engine that simplifies the online guest journey to increase direct bookings.
Namastay.history: Namastay is a European hotel booking engine startup focused on helping hospitality businesses increase direct bookings and reduce dependence on OTAs (online travel agencies). Founded around 2020.
Namastay.summary_abuse: Namastay is a hotel booking engine SaaS platform. It has no notable abuse signal.

NamelessMC.description: NamelessMC is an open-source website software geared towards gaming communities, offering features such as forums and user profiles for managing online presence.
NamelessMC.history: NamelessMC is an open-source CMS and forum software specifically designed for Minecraft gaming communities, first released around 2015 and maintained by a small volunteer team. It provides user profiles, forums, and server integration features.
NamelessMC.summary_abuse: NamelessMC is self-hosted open-source forum software for gaming communities. As self-hosted software, any abuse depends on the individual site operator's moderation. The software itself has no notable abuse signal at the platform level.

Napps.description: Napps is a platform that allows businesses to convert their Shopify and WooCommerce online stores into mobile apps without any coding.
Napps.history: Napps is a no-code mobile app builder founded around 2019 in Portugal that converts Shopify and WooCommerce stores into native mobile apps without requiring coding skills.
Napps.summary_abuse: Napps is a mobile app conversion tool for ecommerce stores. It has no notable abuse signal.

Narrativ.description: Narrativ is a subscription technology platform for brands to acquire new customers through trusted creators.
Narrativ.history: Narrativ was founded in 2013 in New York to build affiliate and content monetization technology that connects publishers and creators with brands. The company raised venture funding and built a subscription-based commerce platform for content creators.
Narrativ.summary_abuse: Narrativ is an affiliate technology platform connecting publishers with brands. Like all affiliate systems it can be used for cookie stuffing or misleading link attribution, but no major documented abuse incidents are known. Legitimate affiliate use dominates.

Narvar.description: Narvar is a customer experience platform that helps retailers inspire long-term customer loyalty, at all steps of the post-purchase journey.
Narvar.history: Narvar was founded in 2012 in San Jose, California, to provide post-purchase customer experience tools for retailers. The company raised significant venture funding and grew to serve major retailers with shipment tracking, returns management, and customer notifications.
Narvar.summary_abuse: Narvar is a B2B post-purchase logistics and notifications platform used by retailers. It has no notable abuse signal in the context of fraud or phishing.

NationBuilder.description: NationBuilder is a Los Angeles based technology start-up that develops content management and customer relationship management (CRM) software.
NationBuilder.history: NationBuilder was founded in 2009 in Los Angeles by Jim Gilliam and Joe Green to provide CRM and website platform tools for political campaigns, nonprofits, and advocacy organizations. The company raised venture funding and expanded from political campaigns to broader community organizing software.
NationBuilder.summary_abuse: NationBuilder is a CRM and website platform primarily used by political campaigns and nonprofits. The platform holds constituent data including voter information, and its use in political campaigns has raised privacy concerns. No significant platform-level abuse (phishing, fraud) has been documented.

NativeForms.description: NativeForms is a tool for creating forms, surveys, and polls across various platforms.
NativeForms.history: NativeForms is a form-building SaaS platform founded around 2019, offering forms, surveys, and polls that work across web and mobile platforms. It is a small independent company.
NativeForms.summary_abuse: NativeForms is a form-building platform. Like all form builders it can be misused for phishing forms or data-harvesting, but no specific platform-level abuse incidents are documented and it is a small niche product.

Nativo.description: Nativo is an advertising technology provider.
Nativo.history: Nativo was founded around 2010 in Los Angeles as a native advertising technology platform that helps publishers monetize content and brands reach audiences through content-matching ad formats. The company raised VC funding and became a significant player in the programmatic native advertising space.
Nativo.summary_abuse: Nativo is an ad tech platform serving native advertising. As with all advertising technology, it can be exploited for malvertising if ad creative review processes are insufficient, though Nativo focuses on premium publisher partnerships. The platform carries typical ad-tech privacy exposure through extensive tracking.

Natural Intelligence.description: Natural Intelligence is a platform facilitating product discovery.
Natural Intelligence.history: Natural Intelligence is an Israeli company founded around 2007 that operates a product discovery and comparison platform helping consumers find products and services. The company operates several comparison and review websites.
Natural Intelligence.summary_abuse: Natural Intelligence operates product comparison and discovery websites. As a comparison and content curation platform it collects significant user behavioral data for targeting. No major documented fraud or phishing abuse incidents are known.

Naukri.description: Naukri is a job board that connects employers and job seekers by listing employment opportunities across industries and locations.
Naukri.history: Naukri was founded in 1997 by Sanjeev Bikhchandani in India as one of the first and largest online job portals in India. It is operated by Info Edge India, a publicly traded company on the NSE and BSE. Naukri has maintained its position as the dominant job platform in India for over 25 years.
Naukri.summary_abuse: Naukri, as a major job portal, is periodically exploited for job-scam listings where fraudulent employers post fake job offers to harvest personal data or money from applicants. The platform implements verification for employers but the scale of the platform means some fraudulent listings appear. Naukri has a reporting mechanism for suspicious job posts.

Navegg.history: Navegg is a Brazilian data and analytics company founded around 2008 that specializes in audience segmentation and behavioral analytics, primarily serving the Brazilian digital advertising market.
Navegg.summary_abuse: Navegg is a Brazilian audience analytics and data platform used for ad targeting. It collects and segments user behavioral data at scale across Brazilian websites, representing significant data broker activity. No specific fraud or phishing abuse incidents are documented.

Naver Analytics.description: Naver Analytics is a Korean based analytics service.
Naver Analytics.history: Naver Analytics is a free website analytics service offered by Naver Corporation, South Korea's dominant internet company. It was developed as a Korean-market analytics tool and is primarily used by Korean website operators who use Naver's broader platform ecosystem.
Naver Analytics.summary_abuse: Naver Analytics is a web analytics service provided by Naver Corporation, South Korea's largest internet company. Like other analytics services it collects user behavior data. It has no notable abuse signal in the context of fraud or phishing.

Naver Maps.description: Naver Maps help develop location-based services which provided by Naver.
Naver Maps.history: Naver Maps is a mapping and location service provided by Naver Corporation, South Korea's leading internet company. The service is dominant in South Korea for local mapping, navigation, and location-based services, and is accessible via the Naver Cloud Platform API for developers.
Naver Maps.summary_abuse: Naver Maps is a mapping API service provided by Naver Corporation. It has no notable abuse signal.

Naver RUA.description: Naver RUA (Real User Analytics) collects performance data from real users to analyze the speed of your website by country, operating system, and browser.
Naver RUA.history: Naver RUA (Real User Analytics) is a performance monitoring service from Naver Corporation that measures website speed from real user sessions, segmented by country, OS, and browser. It is part of the broader Naver Analytics suite.
Naver RUA.summary_abuse: Naver RUA is a real user performance monitoring service from Naver Corporation. It has no notable abuse signal.

Navidium Shipping Protection.description: Navidium is a shipping insurance company that covers packages that are lost, stolen, or damaged in transit. Navidium Shipping Protection is an optional shipping protection service where, in the event of an incident (the order is damaged, lost, or stolen during transit), we will immediately re-ship any damaged, lost, or stolen products.
Navidium Shipping Protection.history: Navidium Shipping Protection is a Shopify app providing optional shipping insurance for ecommerce merchants. It was founded around 2021 as an alternative to carrier-managed shipping protection programs.
Navidium Shipping Protection.summary_abuse: Navidium Shipping Protection is a shipping insurance widget for ecommerce stores. It has no notable abuse signal.

Naviga.description: Naviga is a content creation and management tool, formerly known as Infomaker, designed for media and publishing companies.
Naviga.history: Naviga (formerly Infomaker) is a Swedish software company founded in 1992 that provides content creation and management tools for media and publishing companies. It rebranded from Infomaker to Naviga around 2019 after expanding its product suite for the digital publishing market.
Naviga.summary_abuse: Naviga is a B2B CMS and publishing platform for media companies. It has no notable abuse signal in the context of fraud or phishing.

Navu.description: Navu is a platform that uses AI to enhance B2B websites by delivering targeted messages to visitors, helping improve lead conversion through personalized user engagement.
Navu.history: Navu was founded in 2022 in Palo Alto, California as an AI-powered B2B website personalization and interactive chat platform. It embeds an AI sidebar on business websites to answer visitor questions and guide prospects through the buying process, targeting B2B lead conversion.
Navu.summary_abuse: Navu is a B2B website personalization and AI chat tool with no documented abuse history. The platform is embedded by businesses on their own websites and is not accessible for anonymous content creation or hosting, making it an unlikely abuse vector.

Neat A/B testing.description: Neat A/B Testing is a Shopify app built by NeatShift.
Neat A/B testing.history: Neat A/B Testing is a Shopify app developed by NeatShift for ecommerce merchants to run split tests on their Shopify store. It is a small niche Shopify ecosystem app.
Neat A/B testing.summary_abuse: Neat A/B Testing is a Shopify app for split testing. It has no notable abuse signal.

Nebula Sites.description: Nebula Sites is a platform that allows users to create, host, and manage websites.
Nebula Sites.history: Nebula Sites is a website creation and hosting SaaS platform. No notable founding year or ownership history is publicly available.
Nebula Sites.summary_abuse: Nebula Sites is a website builder and hosting platform with a free tier, which carries moderate abuse risk since page builders with free hosting can be used for phishing or scam landing pages. No specific documented abuse incidents are known.

Need Street.description: Need Street is a web and mobile platform that connects healthcare providers with patients, enabling digital communication and management of healthcare services.
Need Street.history: Need Street is a healthcare technology platform that connects patients with providers through digital communication and appointment scheduling tools. It is a small startup in the health tech space.
Need Street.summary_abuse: Need Street is a healthcare patient-provider communication platform. It has no notable abuse signal.

Neexa.description: Neexa is an AI-powered inquiry and sales agent designed to handle customer queries.
Neexa.history: Neexa is an AI-powered sales agent SaaS founded in 2023 by Ethan Bampaire and Simon Nakibinge through Campaignity Technologies, based in Uganda. The product targets B2C businesses with automated customer inquiry and sales handling.
Neexa.summary_abuse: Neexa has no notable abuse signal. It is a sales automation chatbot aimed at legitimate B2C businesses, with no documented use in phishing or fraud contexts.

Negate.description: Negate is a security service that provides premium bot protection for websites, blocking malicious bots in real time.
Negate.history: Negate is a bot protection and security SaaS service that blocks malicious bots in real time. It is a small security startup with limited public history.
Negate.summary_abuse: Negate is a bot protection security service. It has no notable abuse signal; its purpose is to defend websites against abusive bot traffic.

Nelio Testing.description: Nelio Testing is a plugin that allows testing everything on a WordPress site.
Nelio Testing.history: Nelio Testing is a WordPress A/B testing plugin created by Nelio Software, a Spanish software company founded around 2013. The plugin enables WordPress site owners to test headlines, pages, and themes without requiring developer skills.
Nelio Testing.summary_abuse: Nelio Testing is a WordPress A/B testing plugin. It has no notable abuse signal.

Nemu.description: Nemu is a platform that enables end-to-end tracking to monitor and optimize campaign performance, supporting improved profitability.
Nemu.history: Nemu is a campaign performance analytics SaaS platform. No publicly available founding year or notable corporate history.
Nemu.summary_abuse: Nemu has no notable abuse signal. It is an analytics platform for campaign tracking with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

NEO - Omnichannel Commerce Platform.description: NEO is an ecommerce software that manages multiple online stores.
NEO - Omnichannel Commerce Platform.history: NEO is the omnichannel commerce platform developed by JET e-Business, a Brazilian e-commerce company based in Ribeirão Preto with approximately 20 years of experience. JET built the NEO platform based on experience with more than 15,000 virtual stores, offering multi-store management, inventory, and marketing tools in a SaaS model.
NEO - Omnichannel Commerce Platform.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal. NEO is a Brazilian B2B e-commerce SaaS platform. As a hosted storefront provider it carries standard e-commerce hosting risk, but no documented abuse incidents are reported.

NeoAsist.description: NeoAssist is an omnichannel customer service platform from Brazil focused on improving brand visibility across search engines and AI-driven platforms such as Google and GPT-based systems.
NeoAsist.history: NeoAsist (NeoAssist) is a Brazilian omnichannel customer service platform founded around 2011. It serves Brazilian brands with customer support software including live chat, email management, and AI-driven support tools.
NeoAsist.summary_abuse: NeoAsist is a Brazilian customer service and live chat platform. It has no notable abuse signal.

Neon CRM.description: Neon CRM, a Neon One company, is a cloud-based customer relationship management (CRM) software suite for nonprofits of all sizes. Applications include fundraising management, donor management, membership management, event registration and management, customised reporting, and more.
Neon CRM.history: Neon One was formed in 2018 through the consolidation of four nonprofit technology companies backed by FTV Capital, a growth equity firm. The Chicago-based company serves as an umbrella organisation offering a suite of tools including Neon CRM, a fundraising and donor management platform for nonprofits, alongside event management and membership tools. Blue Star Innovation Partners also invested in the company.
Neon CRM.summary_abuse: Neon CRM is a donor and fundraising management platform for nonprofits with no documented abuse history. It is a subscription-gated B2B tool used by registered nonprofit organisations; there is no anonymous access or consumer-facing content hosting that would attract phishing or fraud activity.

Neos CMS.history: Neos CMS is an open-source content management system created in 2012 as a fork of TYPO3 FLOW (later renamed Neos Flow). It was developed by the Neos project community, primarily with German and Austrian contributions, and positioned as a modern, enterprise-ready alternative to traditional PHP CMSes.
Neos CMS.summary_abuse: Neos CMS is a self-hosted open-source CMS. It has no notable abuse signal at the platform level.

Neos Flow.history: Neos Flow (originally TYPO3 FLOW) is an open-source PHP web application framework first released around 2008 by the TYPO3 community. It was split into its own project as Neos Flow and underpins the Neos CMS. It emphasizes clean architecture and dependency injection.
Neos Flow.summary_abuse: Neos Flow is a PHP web framework with no consumer-facing surface. It has no notable abuse signal.

Neowize.description: Neowize is a platform that applies artificial intelligence and data science to enhance analytics and decision-making in ecommerce.
Neowize.history: Neowize is an Israeli ecommerce AI and analytics platform founded around 2016 that uses data science to improve decision-making for online retailers.
Neowize.summary_abuse: Neowize is an ecommerce analytics and AI decision-support platform. It has no notable abuse signal.

Nepcha Analytics.description: Nepcha Analytics is a privacy-focused analytics platform that collects and processes website usage data without relying on cookies or personal tracking, ensuring compliance with modern data protection standards.
Nepcha Analytics.history: Nepcha Analytics is a privacy-focused web analytics platform founded around 2021 that tracks website metrics without cookies or personal data collection, targeting users seeking GDPR-compliant analytics alternatives.
Nepcha Analytics.summary_abuse: Nepcha Analytics is a cookieless privacy-focused analytics platform. It has no notable abuse signal.

Nepso.history: Nepso is a CMS platform developed by Nepso Digital Agency, an Iranian web design and development agency. No notable ownership changes or founding year is publicly documented.
Nepso.summary_abuse: Nepso has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche CMS used by a small Iranian digital agency for client websites, with no documented abuse in phishing or fraud contexts.

Neshan.description: Neshan is an Iranian route-finding tool that provides users with navigation and mapping services for travel across the country.
Neshan.history: Neshan is an Iranian navigation and mapping platform founded around 2014 that serves the Iranian market with route-finding, GPS navigation, and local business discovery. It is one of the leading mapping services in Iran.
Neshan.summary_abuse: Neshan is an Iranian mapping and navigation service. It has no notable abuse signal.

Nestify.description: Nestify is a fully managed WordPress hosting platform that runs on AWS graviton processors.
Nestify.history: Nestify is a managed WordPress hosting service built on AWS Graviton processors, founded around 2017 and targeting WordPress site owners seeking performance and managed support.
Nestify.summary_abuse: Nestify is a managed WordPress hosting platform. It has no notable abuse signal specific to it, though hosting platforms in general can be used to host malicious content.

NET-RESULTS.description: NET-RESULTS is a marketing automation platform for the data-driven marketer.
NET-RESULTS.history: NET-RESULTS is a marketing automation platform for mid-market companies, founded in 2003 and based in Denver, Colorado. The company has approximately 20 employees and around $3M in revenue. It recently rebranded as Paminga. No major acquisition or ownership events documented.
NET-RESULTS.summary_abuse: NET-RESULTS is a marketing automation platform providing mass email campaigns and lead scoring. Like other email marketing platforms, it can be misused for spam. However, no specific documented abuse incidents are associated with the platform, and it serves a B2B audience with standard AUP terms.

Netcore Cloud.description: Netcore Cloud is a globally recognised marketing technology SaaS company.
Netcore Cloud.history: Netcore Cloud (originally Netcore Solutions) was founded in 2001 in Mumbai, India and grew into a global marketing technology company offering email marketing, customer data platforms, and omnichannel engagement tools. The company serves large enterprises across multiple regions.
Netcore Cloud.summary_abuse: Netcore Cloud is a large marketing automation and email delivery platform. Like all bulk email and marketing automation providers, it can be misused for spam campaigns, though Netcore maintains industry-standard abuse monitoring. The platform's mass-email capabilities are the primary abuse-risk vector.

Netdeal.description: Netdeal is a marketing automation platform.
Netdeal.history: Netdeal is a Brazilian marketing automation platform serving mainly Brazilian businesses. Limited public information is available about its founding or ownership history.
Netdeal.summary_abuse: Netdeal is a Brazilian marketing automation and email marketing platform. Like all bulk email platforms it carries spam risk, but no specific documented abuse incidents are known.

Nethouse Website Builder.description: Nethouse Website Builder is a platform enabling individuals to craft websites, online stores, or landing pages independently, without the need for programmers or designers.
Nethouse Website Builder.history: Nethouse Website Builder is a Russian website builder platform founded around 2012 that allows individuals and small businesses to create websites, online stores, and landing pages without coding.
Nethouse Website Builder.summary_abuse: Nethouse Website Builder is a Russian website-builder and hosting platform. Like other low-cost website builders, it carries some risk of being used to host phishing pages or scam storefronts, and its Russian origin may limit abuse enforcement responsiveness for global security teams.

Netlify.description: Netlify providers hosting and server-less backend services for web applications and static websites.
Netlify.history: Netlify was founded in 2014 by Mathias Biilmann and Chris Bach in San Francisco to pioneer the JAMstack hosting model for static sites and serverless apps. The company raised substantial VC funding and became the defining platform for modern frontend deployment. In 2023, Netlify acquired Gatsby Inc., further expanding its composable web capabilities.
Netlify.summary_abuse: Netlify's free tier with custom subdomain support has been documented by security researchers as occasionally used for phishing page hosting due to the ease of deployment. Netlify publishes an AUP and responds to abuse reports, but the free tier's permissive nature means it appears in threat intelligence feeds. Abuse is a small fraction of overall use and the vendor is generally responsive to takedown requests.

Netlify Create.description: Netlify Create (formerly Stackbit) is a visual experience platform for building decoupled websites.
Netlify Create.history: Netlify Create was previously known as Stackbit, an independent visual website editing tool acquired by Netlify in 2023. It provides a visual editing experience for headless and composable website architectures.
Netlify Create.summary_abuse: Netlify Create is a visual web editing platform for composable architectures. It has no notable abuse signal.

Netlify Forms.description: Netlify Forms is a serverless form handling solution for static websites.
Netlify Forms.history: Netlify Forms is a serverless form handling feature built into the Netlify platform, launched as part of Netlify's core offering since 2014. It enables static sites to handle form submissions without a backend server.
Netlify Forms.summary_abuse: Netlify Forms is a serverless form processing feature. Like all form endpoints, it can theoretically be used in phishing campaigns, though Netlify's AUP and abuse team address such misuse. No notable specific incidents are documented.

Neto.description: Neto is the only Australian B2B and multi-channel ecommerce platform that provides an all-in-one solution for ecommerce, POS, inventory management, order management, and shipping labelling.
Neto.history: Neto is an Australian ecommerce and retail management platform founded in 2009, initially targeting Australian B2B merchants and multi-channel retailers. The company was acquired by Maropost in 2021 and rebranded as Maropost Commerce Cloud.
Neto.summary_abuse: Neto is a B2B ecommerce and retail management platform. It has no notable abuse signal.

Netop.description: Netop is a platform that provides remote support and chat solutions for secure communication and technical assistance.
Netop.history: Netop was founded in 1981 in Denmark and has a long history in remote access and support software, originally known for educational software before pivoting to enterprise remote support tools. The company has been acquired and rebranded multiple times, with its remote support products widely used in IT support workflows.
Netop.summary_abuse: Netop is a remote access and support platform. Remote access tools are sometimes misused by tech-support scammers to gain unauthorized access to victim computers, though Netop targets legitimate enterprise IT support workflows and requires user consent for connections.

Netrox.description: Netrox SC is a Russian online consultant technology platform that provides live chat and customer support tools for websites.
Netrox.history: Netrox SC is a Russian live chat and online consultant platform founded around 2010, primarily serving the Russian-speaking market with website chat support tools.
Netrox.summary_abuse: Netrox SC is a Russian live chat platform. Its Russian origin may limit enforcement responsiveness for global abuse reports. No specific documented fraud or phishing incidents are known.

NetSuite.description: NetSuite is a cloud-based enterprise resource planning (ERP) software suite that provides a comprehensive set of business management applications, including financial management, CRM, ecommerce, and more.
NetSuite.history: NetSuite was founded in 1998 by Evan Goldberg as NetLedger and rebranded to NetSuite in 2001, positioning itself as one of the first cloud ERP systems. Oracle acquired NetSuite in 2016 for approximately $9.3 billion. It remains the world's most widely deployed cloud ERP platform, used by over 37,000 organizations globally.
NetSuite.summary_abuse: NetSuite is an enterprise ERP platform used by tens of thousands of businesses worldwide. As backend business software with no consumer-facing surface, it has no notable abuse signal.

Nette Framework.history: Nette Framework is an open-source PHP web framework created by David Grudl of the Czech Republic, first released around 2004. It became popular in Czech and Slovak web development communities and is known for its elegant architecture and strong security features.
Nette Framework.summary_abuse: Nette Framework is a PHP web application framework with no consumer-facing surface. It has no notable abuse signal.

Netvibes.description: Netvibes is a platform for social media monitoring, analytics, and real-time alerts.
Netvibes.history: Netvibes was founded in 2005 in France as one of the first personalized start-page and RSS dashboard services. After initial popularity, it pivoted to social media monitoring and enterprise analytics. Dassault Systèmes acquired Netvibes in 2012 to enhance its collaborative intelligence platform.
Netvibes.summary_abuse: Netvibes is a social media monitoring and analytics dashboard. It has no notable abuse signal.

Network for Good.description: Network for Good is an American certified B Corporation software company that offers fundraising software and coaching for charities and non-profit organisations.
Network for Good.history: Network for Good was founded in 2001 as a nonprofit that later became a certified B Corporation, providing fundraising software and coaching specifically designed for small to mid-size nonprofits and charities in the United States.
Network for Good.summary_abuse: Network for Good is a nonprofit fundraising software platform. It handles charitable donations and is designed for legitimate nonprofits. Charitable giving platforms can be misused by fake charities to solicit donations fraudulently, though Network for Good screens organizations. No major documented platform-level fraud incidents are known.

Neve.description: Neve is a super-fast, easily customizable, multi-purpose theme that works perfectly with Gutenberg and the most popular page builders as well as WooCommerce
Neve.history: Neve is a WordPress theme developed by Themeisle, a Romanian WordPress products company, first released in 2018. It became one of the most popular free WordPress themes due to its speed, compatibility with major page builders, and WooCommerce support.
Neve.summary_abuse: Neve is a WordPress theme. It has no notable abuse signal.

New Relic.description: New Relic is a SaaS offering that focuses on performance and availability monitoring.
New Relic.history: New Relic was founded in 2008 by Lew Cirne in San Francisco and became one of the foundational application performance monitoring (APM) companies in the cloud era. The company went public on the NYSE in 2014. In 2023, New Relic was taken private in a ~$6.5 billion acquisition by Francisco Partners and TPG.
New Relic.summary_abuse: New Relic is an enterprise observability and APM platform. It is backend monitoring software with no consumer-facing surface. It has no notable abuse signal.

New UI.description: New UI is a framework designed for developing user interfaces for SaaS applications and websites.
New UI.history: New UI is a UI framework for building SaaS applications and websites. No notable founding year or corporate history is publicly documented.
New UI.summary_abuse: New UI has no notable abuse signal. It is a UI framework for developers with no consumer-facing hosting surface.

Newo.description: Newo is an AI platform offering automated solutions for sales, reception, and concierge services across various industries.
Newo.history: Newo was co-founded in 2023 in San Francisco by Luba Rein, David Yang, Alex Novitsky, and Viacheslav Seledkin as an AI agent platform for SMB front-desk automation. In February 2026 the company raised a $25M Series A, bringing total funding to $32M.
Newo.summary_abuse: Newo has no notable abuse signal. It is an AI voice and text agent platform for SMB receptionists, with no consumer-facing content hosting or anonymous signup that would attract fraudsters.

Newspack.description: Newspack is an open-source publishing platform built on WordPress for small to medium sized news organizations. It is an “opinionated” platform that stakes out clear, best-practice positions on technology, design, and business practice for news publishers.
Newspack.history: Newspack was launched in 2019 as a Google News Initiative-funded open-source publishing platform built on WordPress, developed by Automattic to help small and medium news organizations with modern digital publishing. It provides opinionated configurations for news industry best practices.
Newspack.summary_abuse: Newspack is an open-source WordPress publishing platform for news organizations. It has no notable abuse signal.

Newspack by Automattic.description: Automattic's Newspack service is an all-in-one platform designed for small and medium-sized news organizations that simplifies publishing and drives audience and revenue right out of the box.
Newspack by Automattic.history: Newspack by Automattic is the managed hosting service for the Newspack open-source platform, providing a fully managed publishing infrastructure for small and medium news organizations. Automattic (the company behind WordPress.com) developed and maintains this service.
Newspack by Automattic.summary_abuse: Newspack by Automattic is a managed hosting platform for news publishers. It has no notable abuse signal.

NewStore.description: NewStore is the only integrated platform offering omnichannel solutions for stores and consumers.
NewStore.history: NewStore was founded in 2015 in Berlin by Stephan Schambach (who previously founded Demandware). The company provides an omnichannel retail platform that unifies online and in-store operations for fashion and specialty retailers, with significant VC backing.
NewStore.summary_abuse: NewStore is an enterprise omnichannel retail platform. It has no notable abuse signal.

Newt.description: Newt is a headless CMS that allows you to create and manage structured content and distribute it to a variety of channels.
Newt.history: Newt was founded in 2021 by Kazuki Takahashi and Youhei Meguro in Tokyo, Japan, as a cloud-based headless CMS designed for structured content management with API distribution. The company raised a $733K seed round from XTech Ventures in July 2022 and launched a free plan in March 2022. The official Newt website has announced service termination on November 24, 2026.
Newt.summary_abuse: Newt is a Japanese headless CMS with no documented abuse history. As the service is scheduled to shut down in late 2026, it is effectively no longer active for new adoptions. The API-based CMS model without consumer-facing content hosting provides minimal abuse surface.

Nexcess.description: Nexcess is a web hosting service.
Nexcess.history: Nexcess was founded in 2000 and became known for managed WordPress and Magento hosting. The company was acquired by Liquid Web, a managed hosting provider, around 2016. It focuses on high-performance hosting for WooCommerce, WordPress, and Magento sites.
Nexcess.summary_abuse: Nexcess is a managed hosting provider. Like all hosting services it can be used to host malicious content, but Nexcess maintains abuse policies and focuses on premium managed customers rather than anonymous low-cost shared hosting which is more commonly abused.

Nexive.description: Nexive is a postal operator in Italy.
Nexive.history: Nexive is an Italian private postal operator offering parcel and mail delivery services. It is part of the Poste Italiane ecosystem and operates in the Italian logistics market.
Nexive.summary_abuse: Nexive is an Italian postal and shipping carrier. It has no notable abuse signal.

NexMind.description: NexMind is a platform offering advanced analytics tools for businesses to make informed decisions and foster growth.
NexMind.history: NexMind AI was founded in 2019 in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, by Francis Lui who had previously founded Nexus Mediaworks in 2005, Malaysia's first SEO company. The company pivoted to AI-driven SEO and content generation, building a multilingual platform that uses NLP and big data to help businesses rank on search engines and generate optimised content.
NexMind.summary_abuse: NexMind is a Malaysian AI-powered SEO and content generation platform. AI content generation tools carry a moderate risk of being used to produce thin or spammy SEO content at scale, though NexMind is a subscription B2B product with no documented involvement in spam campaigns or malicious activity.

Next Basket.description: Next Basket is an AI-based software platform designed to create online stores and warehouses for products.
Next Basket.history: Founded in 2021 by entrepreneur Borislav Malinov and headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, Next Basket is an AI-powered ecommerce SaaS platform that achieved EUR 28 million in sales by 2024 and expanded to 8 countries across 4 continents.
Next Basket.summary_abuse: Next Basket is an ecommerce platform builder targeting small and mid-sized businesses. Like other storefront builders it could theoretically be used to stand up fake shops, but no documented abuse pattern has been reported for this platform specifically. Its B2B focus and sales verification process reduce anonymous misuse risk.

Next Total.description: Next is leveraging the expertise, infrastructure and software it has developed for its own online business to provide a third-party ecommerce outsourcing service named Total Platform.
Next Total.history: Next Total Platform is an ecommerce outsourcing service offered by Next plc, the large British fashion retailer founded in 1864. Next built proprietary ecommerce infrastructure for its own retail business and began offering this as a managed third-party service to other brands, leveraging its logistics, technology, and fulfilment capabilities.
Next Total.summary_abuse: Next Total Platform is an enterprise ecommerce outsourcing service from Next plc with no notable abuse signal. It targets established retail brands and operates with enterprise contracts, making it an unlikely target for fraud or misuse.

Next.js.description: Next.js is a React framework for developing single page Javascript applications.
Next.js.history: Next.js was created by Vercel (formerly Zeit) and first released in October 2016. It quickly became the leading React framework for server-side rendering and static site generation. Vercel raised significant venture capital and built a commercial hosting platform around Next.js, which remains MIT-licensed open source maintained primarily by the Vercel team.
Next.js.summary_abuse: Next.js has no notable abuse signal. It is a backend web framework with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Next.js App Router.description: The Next.js App Router is a new paradigm for building applications using React's latest features.
Next.js App Router.history: The Next.js App Router was introduced in Next.js 13 (2022) by Vercel as a new paradigm for building React applications using the latest React Server Components feature. It coexists with the older Pages Router and represents the recommended direction for new Next.js projects.
Next.js App Router.summary_abuse: The Next.js App Router has no notable abuse signal. It is a server-side rendering paradigm within a developer framework with no consumer-facing surface.

Next.js Page Router SSG.history: Next.js Page Router SSG (Static Site Generation) is a rendering strategy available in the Next.js Pages Router since its early versions, allowing pages to be pre-rendered at build time. Part of the original Next.js offering from Vercel, it remains in active maintenance though the newer App Router is now the recommended approach.
Next.js Page Router SSG.summary_abuse: Next.js Page Router SSG has no notable abuse signal. It is a static site generation pattern within a developer framework with no consumer-facing surface fraudsters would target.

Next.js Page Router SSR.history: Next.js Page Router SSR (Server-Side Rendering) is a rendering strategy available in the Next.js Pages Router since its early versions, enabling pages to be rendered on the server per request. Part of the original Next.js offering from Vercel, the Pages Router remains in long-term maintenance while the App Router is now the recommended approach for new projects.
Next.js Page Router SSR.summary_abuse: Next.js Page Router SSR has no notable abuse signal. It is a server-side rendering pattern within a developer framework with no consumer-facing surface fraudsters would target.

NextAuth.js.description: NextAuth.js is a complete open-source authentication solution for Next.js applications.
NextAuth.js.history: NextAuth.js was created around 2020 as an open-source authentication library for Next.js. In 2022 it was rebranded as Auth.js to support multiple frameworks beyond Next.js. It is community-maintained with no major corporate sponsor.
NextAuth.js.summary_abuse: NextAuth.js has no notable abuse signal. It is a developer authentication library with no consumer-facing surface.

Nextcloud.description: Nextcloud is a suite of client-server software for creating and using file hosting services.
Nextcloud.history: Nextcloud was founded in 2016 by Frank Karlitschek, who forked ownCloud (which he had previously founded) due to disagreements over the business model and open-source approach. Nextcloud GmbH is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany and focuses strongly on privacy-respecting self-hosted file sync and collaboration.
Nextcloud.summary_abuse: Nextcloud has minimal abuse signal. As a self-hosted file sharing and collaboration platform, it is occasionally used by individuals to host illegal content on poorly-administered instances, but this is rare relative to legitimate enterprise and personal use. Nextcloud GmbH maintains a security disclosure policy and actively patches vulnerabilities.

Nextdoor Ads.description: Nextdoor Ads is an easy-to-use expansion of Nextdoor’s proprietary self-serve campaign management platform, designed to help small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) advertise on Nextdoor.
Nextdoor Ads.history: Nextdoor was founded in 2011 in San Francisco as a social network for neighborhoods. Nextdoor Ads (also called Neighborhood Ad Center) is the advertising platform built on top of Nextdoor to let SMBs run targeted local ads. Nextdoor went public via SPAC in 2021.
Nextdoor Ads.summary_abuse: Nextdoor Ads is an advertising pixel and campaign platform tied to the Nextdoor social network. It collects user behavioral data for ad targeting. There are no notable reports of it being weaponized for fraud or scams, but like all ad-tech platforms it contributes to behavioral profiling and cross-site tracking.

NextGEN Gallery.description: NextGEN Gallery is a free open-source image management plugin for the WordPress content management system.
NextGEN Gallery.history: NextGEN Gallery is a WordPress image gallery plugin originally released around 2007 and later acquired and maintained by Imagely. It is one of the most widely installed gallery plugins in the WordPress ecosystem with over 1 million active installations.
NextGEN Gallery.summary_abuse: NextGEN Gallery has no notable abuse signal. It is a WordPress image gallery plugin used for legitimate website photo display.

Nextra.description: Nextra is Next.js based static site generator.
Nextra.history: Nextra is a Next.js-based static site generator created by the Vercel-adjacent community around 2021-2022, primarily used for documentation sites. It gained popularity as a documentation framework for open-source projects.
Nextra.summary_abuse: Nextra has no notable abuse signal. It is a static site generator framework with no consumer-facing surface.

Nextsale.description: Nextsale is a conversion optimisation platform that provides social proof and urgency tools for ecommerce websites.
Nextsale.history: Nextsale is a conversion optimization SaaS platform providing social proof notifications and urgency tools for e-commerce sites. It is a small startup with no notable acquisition or ownership events.
Nextsale.summary_abuse: Nextsale provides social proof and urgency notification widgets. These types of tools can be used to create false scarcity or fake social proof to manipulate users, which is a mild dark pattern risk, but no specific abuse incidents are documented for this vendor.

NextUI.description: NextUI allows you to make beautiful, modern, and fast websites/applications regardless of your design experience, created with React.js and Stitches, based on React Aria and inspired by Vuesax.
NextUI.history: NextUI is a React UI component library created around 2021, built on top of React Aria and Tailwind CSS. It provides pre-built accessible components for React applications. In 2023 the project released NextUI v2, switching from Stitches to Tailwind CSS as its styling engine.
NextUI.summary_abuse: NextUI has no notable abuse signal. It is a React component library with no consumer-facing surface.

NexusPHP.description: NexusPHP is an open-sourced private tracker script written in PHP.
NexusPHP.history: NexusPHP is an open-source private BitTorrent tracker script written in PHP, originating from Chinese developer communities around the mid-2000s. It is widely used to power private torrent tracking sites, particularly in Chinese-speaking regions.
NexusPHP.summary_abuse: NexusPHP is the codebase behind many private BitTorrent tracker sites. While private trackers typically share copyrighted content without authorization, NexusPHP itself is neutral infrastructure. Its primary use case enables copyright infringement, and many sites running it distribute pirated software, movies, and games. Anti-abuse posture depends entirely on the individual site operator.

NexusPIPE.description: NexusPIPE is a ADC and DDoS mitigation Company.
NexusPIPE.history: NexusPIPE is a small DDoS mitigation and application delivery controller (ADC) company. It is a niche infrastructure provider with no notable acquisition or ownership events on public record.
NexusPIPE.summary_abuse: NexusPIPE is a DDoS mitigation and ADC infrastructure provider with no notable abuse signal. Infrastructure of this type is used for legitimate traffic scrubbing and content delivery.

Nexxt.description: Nexxt is a job finder-based system designed to help users search for employment opportunities by matching skills and preferences with job listings across various industries.
Nexxt.history: Nexxt is a job search and professional networking platform primarily serving US job seekers. It has operated as an employment marketplace for many years with no notable ownership changes on public record.
Nexxt.summary_abuse: Nexxt is a job board and professional network. Job platforms can be targeted for fake job listing scams that phish for personal information, though no specific high-profile incidents have been documented for Nexxt. The risk is at the low end relative to the overall job board category.

Nexxtmove.description: Nexxtmove is a marketing platform for real estate agencies that provides targeted, personalized communication tools designed to reach the appropriate audience and support improved campaign outcomes.
Nexxtmove.history: Nexxtmove is a marketing automation platform targeting real estate agencies, providing personalized communication tools. It is a small niche SaaS startup with no notable ownership changes on public record.
Nexxtmove.summary_abuse: Nexxtmove is a niche real estate marketing platform with no notable abuse signal. It operates in a regulated industry sector.

Ngage Live Chat.description: Ngage Live Chat is an industry-specific live chat solution that converts website visitors into clients.
Ngage Live Chat.history: Ngage Live Chat is a live chat and lead generation service tailored for professional service industries such as legal, real estate, and home services. It provides staffed chat agents rather than just software. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Ngage Live Chat.summary_abuse: Ngage Live Chat is a staffed live chat service for professional industries with no notable abuse signal. It serves as a lead generation tool for regulated service categories.

nghttpx - HTTP/2 proxy.history: nghttpx is an HTTP/2 reverse proxy tool and part of the nghttp2 library suite created by Tatsuhiro Tsujikawa. It provides HTTP/2 proxying, SSL termination, and load balancing capabilities.
nghttpx - HTTP/2 proxy.summary_abuse: nghttpx has no notable abuse signal; it is a backend HTTP/2 proxy tool with no consumer-facing surface.

Nginx.description: Nginx is a web server that can also be used as a reverse proxy, load balancer, mail proxy and HTTP cache.
Nginx.history: Nginx was created by Igor Sysoev and first released in 2004 to handle the C10k problem. It became one of the most widely deployed web servers globally. In 2011, Nginx Inc was founded to commercialize the project. In 2019, F5 Networks acquired Nginx Inc for approximately $670 million, making it a subsidiary. The open-source core remains under the BSD 2-clause license.
Nginx.summary_abuse: Nginx has no meaningful abuse signal as a technology itself. It is a backend web server and reverse proxy that has no consumer-facing surface. When abused sites run on Nginx, the responsibility lies with the site operator, not with Nginx.

Niagahoster.description: Niagahoster is a web hosting service for small and medium enterprises. It provides shared hosting, WordPress hosting, Virtual Private Server (VPS), and more.
Niagahoster.history: Niagahoster is an Indonesian web hosting provider founded to serve the local SME market. It offers shared hosting, VPS, and domain registration primarily targeting Indonesian businesses and individuals.
Niagahoster.summary_abuse: Niagahoster is a regional Indonesian web hosting provider. Like most shared hosting services, it may occasionally host spam or phishing content on poorly-managed customer accounts, but no specific public abuse incidents have been documented for this provider.

Nibiru Ecommerce.description: Nibiru Ecommerce is a Uruguay-based SaaS platform for building and managing online stores with no sales commissions, offering integrations, scalability, and full control from day one.
Nibiru Ecommerce.history: Nibiru Ecommerce is a Uruguay-based SaaS platform for building online stores, targeting the Latin American market. It is an early-stage startup with no notable ownership changes on record.
Nibiru Ecommerce.summary_abuse: Nibiru Ecommerce is a small e-commerce platform builder with no notable abuse signal. Its geographic focus on Latin America and small user base reduce its risk profile.

Nicepage.description: Nicepage is a free website building tool that requires no coding skills and integrates seamlessly with all leading CMS systems.
Nicepage.history: Nicepage is a no-code website builder offering both an online SaaS editor and desktop application. It was founded around 2017 and positioned as a free drag-and-drop alternative to traditional website builders, integrating with WordPress and Joomla.
Nicepage.summary_abuse: Nicepage is a website builder with a free tier that allows custom domain publishing. Website builders offering free hosting with custom domains are occasionally used to create phishing landing pages or scam storefronts, though no specific incidents have been documented for Nicepage specifically.

Nido Tecnologia.description: Nido Tecnologia is a platform that provides software solutions for the real estate industry in Brazil.
Nido Tecnologia.history: Nido Tecnologia is a Brazilian SaaS platform providing software solutions for the real estate industry. It is a small startup serving the local Brazilian property management and agency market.
Nido Tecnologia.summary_abuse: Nido Tecnologia is a niche Brazilian real estate SaaS with no notable abuse signal.

Nidux.description: Nidux is an ecommerce platform catering to small and medium businesses.
Nidux.history: Nidux was founded in 2011 in San José, Costa Rica as a cloud-based ecommerce and omnichannel platform targeting SMEs across Latin America. The platform has grown to support over 2,600 active online stores across the region, offering social commerce, mobile commerce, and traditional ecommerce integrations.
Nidux.summary_abuse: Nidux is a Latin American ecommerce platform used by SMEs to build online stores. Like other ecommerce platform providers, it carries some residual risk of being used to host fake storefronts, but there is no documented pattern of significant abuse or fraud associated with Nidux specifically.

Nift.description: Nift is a marketing program for pools of local businesses. Businesses give Nift gift cards to thank and reward their customers for taking actions, like signing up for a newsletter, referring a friend, or making a purchase.
Nift.history: Nift is a B2B marketing platform that allows businesses to offer digital gift cards as loyalty rewards to customers. It operates as a network of local businesses and merchants, founded around 2015 in the US.
Nift.summary_abuse: Nift operates a gift card and loyalty reward network for businesses. Gift card platforms can be targets for fraud, with bad actors sometimes exploiting free or promotional gift card offers. No specific high-profile abuse incidents have been documented for Nift.

NightsBridge.description: NightsBridge is a SaaS platform that provides booking and channel management tools for accommodation providers.
NightsBridge.history: NightsBridge is a South African SaaS platform providing booking management and channel management tools for accommodation providers such as guesthouses, B&Bs, and small hotels. It has been operating since the early 2000s and is one of the leading systems in the South African hospitality market.
NightsBridge.summary_abuse: NightsBridge is a hospitality booking management platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves legitimate accommodation businesses in the Southern African market.

Niice.description: Niice is a Dutch marketing automation platform that streamlines campaign management, customer engagement, and data-driven communication processes.
Niice.history: Niice is a Dutch digital marketing agency and marketing automation platform based in Groenlo, in the Achterhoek region of the Netherlands. It serves Dutch SME clients with digital marketing, marketing automation, SEO, web design, and conversion optimisation services built on Spotler integration.
Niice.summary_abuse: Niice is a small Dutch digital marketing agency offering marketing automation software. It has no documented abuse history and serves local Dutch SME clients with marketing services rather than providing anonymous or consumer-facing hosted content.

Nimbata.description: Nimbata is a call tracking and conversion attribution platform designed to help marketing professionals, teams, and agencies make informed decisions to improve campaign performance.
Nimbata.history: Nimbata is a call tracking and conversion attribution SaaS platform founded in Greece around 2016. It helps marketing teams attribute phone call conversions to online campaigns. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Nimbata.summary_abuse: Nimbata is a call tracking and analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by marketing teams to attribute phone inquiries to advertising campaigns.

Nimbu.description: Nimbu is a platform that integrates content management, e-commerce, and a scalable cloud database into a single system.
Nimbu.history: Nimbu is a small Belgian company headquartered in Leuven, Belgium, offering an integrated platform combining CMS, ecommerce, and a cloud database under one system. With a team of approximately 5 people, the company built native mobile SDKs and provides a developer-friendly platform for building sites and apps. No notable funding or ownership changes have been documented.
Nimbu.summary_abuse: Nimbu is a niche Belgian CMS and ecommerce platform with no documented abuse history. It is a small-scale professional tool with no notable consumer-facing features that would attract phishing operators.

Ninchat.description: Ninchat is a secure messaging platform designed for organizations to facilitate encrypted communication and collaboration.
Ninchat.history: Ninchat is a Finnish secure messaging and customer service platform designed for organizations requiring encrypted communications. It has been operating since the early 2010s as an enterprise and public sector communication tool.
Ninchat.summary_abuse: Ninchat is a secure enterprise messaging platform with no notable abuse signal. Its focus on organizational encrypted communications for enterprise and public sector clients reduces its risk profile.

Ninja Forms.description: Ninja Forms is the WordPress form builder.
Ninja Forms.history: Ninja Forms is a WordPress form builder plugin created by Saturday Drive and first released in 2011. It became one of the most popular WordPress form plugins with millions of installations. Saturday Drive offers both a free core plugin and paid add-ons.
Ninja Forms.summary_abuse: Ninja Forms has no notable abuse signal. It is a WordPress form building plugin with no direct consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

NinjaCat.description: NinjaCat is a marketing analytics platform that aggregates, analyzes, and reports performance data across multiple marketing channels.
NinjaCat.history: NinjaCat is a marketing analytics and reporting platform founded around 2013 in the US. It aggregates data from multiple digital marketing channels and provides automated reporting for marketing agencies. The company has raised venture capital funding.
NinjaCat.summary_abuse: NinjaCat is a marketing analytics and reporting platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves marketing agencies for performance reporting purposes.

NitroPack.description: NitroPack creates optimised HTML cache for fast page loading experience.
NitroPack.history: NitroPack is a Bulgarian web performance optimization SaaS platform founded around 2019. It provides automatic caching, image optimization, code minification, and CDN services primarily for WordPress and other CMS-based sites. It has grown to serve a substantial number of websites globally.
NitroPack.summary_abuse: NitroPack is a web performance and caching service with no notable abuse signal. It serves as a technical optimization layer for websites and has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

NOCNOK.description: NOCNOK is a CRM for real estate agencies that uses AI to automate lead responses, reduce response time, and support sales workflow management.
NOCNOK.history: NOCNOK is a Mexican real estate CRM platform founded in Monterrey, Nuevo Leon. It uses AI to automate lead responses and credit pre-qualification for real estate agencies. The platform serves over 35,000 real estate agents and connects listings to portals and social networks.
NOCNOK.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal. NOCNOK is a B2B real estate CRM serving the Mexican market with no documented abuse patterns.

Nocodelytics.description: Nocodelytics is a tool for tracking clicks, searches, CMS items, and more on your Webflow site. It allows you to monitor user activity and important metrics without any coding required.
Nocodelytics.history: Nocodelytics is an analytics plugin for Webflow sites allowing no-code tracking of clicks and user behavior. Founded around 2021 with no notable corporate events recorded.
Nocodelytics.summary_abuse: Nocodelytics has no notable abuse signal. It is a Webflow analytics add-on with no consumer-facing content hosting or anonymous-access features.

Noddus.description: Noddus offers brands and agencies access to an advanced proprietary content marketing platform automating content production, distribution and analytics.
Noddus.history: Noddus is a content marketing automation platform for brands and agencies. No notable founding year or corporate history is publicly documented.
Noddus.summary_abuse: Noddus has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B content marketing SaaS with no consumer-facing hosting that would attract phishing or scam operators.

Node.js.description: Node.js is an open-source, cross-platform, JavaScript runtime environment that executes JavaScript code outside a web browser.
Node.js.history: Node.js was created by Ryan Dahl and first released in 2009, enabling JavaScript to run server-side on the V8 engine. In 2014 a key fork called io.js emerged due to governance disputes, but the two merged back in 2015 under the newly formed Node.js Foundation. That foundation later merged with the JS Foundation in 2019 to form the OpenJS Foundation, which now governs Node.js.
Node.js.summary_abuse: Node.js has no notable abuse signal as a platform. It is a foundational server-side runtime with no consumer-facing surface. While malware may be written in Node.js, the runtime itself is not a preferred tool for scammers or phishing operators relative to its overwhelming legitimate use.

NodeBB.description: NodeBB forum software is powered by Node.js and built on either a Redis or MongoDB database.
NodeBB.history: NodeBB was founded in 2014 in Toronto, Canada by Julian, Andrew, and Barış, who had previously worked together at a social gaming company. Built on Node.js with MongoDB or Redis, it became a popular open-source forum platform. NodeBB Inc. offers a cloud-hosted version alongside the GPL-3 self-hosted edition.
NodeBB.summary_abuse: NodeBB is an open-source forum platform with no notable systemic abuse signal. Self-hosted forums can be set up for any purpose, but the software itself is a legitimate community tool with an active maintainer. No documented use as phishing infrastructure.

NodePing.description: NodePing is a tool in the Website Monitoring category of a tech stack. NodePing is an open source tool with GitHub stars and GitHub forks.
NodePing.history: NodePing was founded in 2011 by Shawn Parrish in Phoenix, Arizona as a website and server uptime monitoring service. It has remained an independent, bootstrapped product with no notable ownership changes.
NodePing.summary_abuse: NodePing has no notable abuse signal. It is an uptime monitoring service with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Nody.description: Nody is a platform that enables the creation of online shops without requiring programming knowledge.
Nody.history: Nody is a no-code ecommerce platform enabling online shop creation without programming. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Nody.summary_abuse: Nody is a no-code ecommerce builder with a free tier, which could be used to create fake storefronts, but no documented abuse incidents are known.

NoFraud.description: NoFraud is a fraud prevention solution for ecommerce businesses.
NoFraud.history: NoFraud was founded in 2014 in New York as a fraud prevention platform for ecommerce merchants, offering chargeback guarantees and real-time transaction screening. The company has grown through VC funding and named Scott Gifis as CEO in 2023.
NoFraud.summary_abuse: NoFraud is a fraud prevention tool that helps merchants detect and block fraudulent transactions. It has no notable abuse signal as a technology and is not known to be misused in phishing or scam infrastructure.

Nogin.description: Nogin is a platform operating as Commerce-as-a-Service (CaaS), offering cloud-based ecommerce functionalities and infrastructure for seamless integration and development of ecommerce solutions.
Nogin.history: Nogin was originally founded as Branded Online in 2010 in Tustin, California, and rebranded to Nogin as it pivoted to a Commerce-as-a-Service model. The company went public on Nasdaq under ticker NOGN and announced restructuring in 2024.
Nogin.summary_abuse: Nogin has no notable abuse signal. It is an ecommerce infrastructure platform for brands, with no consumer-facing content hosting features relevant to phishing or fraud.

Noibu.description: Noibu helps ecommerce websites detect revenue-impacting errors on their websites and provides them with the information needed to resolve them.
Noibu.history: Noibu is an ecommerce error detection and revenue recovery SaaS, founded around 2019 in Canada. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have been documented.
Noibu.summary_abuse: Noibu has no notable abuse signal. It is a technical error-monitoring tool for ecommerce sites with no consumer-facing hosting features.

Nolt.description: Nolt is a collaborative platform for managing user requests, designed to replace outdated spreadsheets and Trello boards while providing actionable customer insights.
Nolt.history: Nolt was founded in 2018 in Toronto, Canada as a customer feedback and feature request management tool, positioned as a replacement for spreadsheets and Trello boards. It has remained a small bootstrapped product with no notable ownership changes.
Nolt.summary_abuse: Nolt has no notable abuse signal. It is a feedback collection tool for product teams with no consumer-facing public hosting or anonymous sign-up that would attract fraudsters.

Noodle Factory.description: Noodle Factory is a tutor platform that enhances teaching through personalized and engaging student learning experiences.
Noodle Factory.history: Noodle Factory was founded in 2018 in Singapore by Yvonne Soh and Jim Wagstaff as an AI-powered tutoring platform for educators. The company raised a $500K seed round to expand internationally.
Noodle Factory.summary_abuse: Noodle Factory has no notable abuse signal. It is an education technology platform for teachers and institutions with no consumer-facing content hosting or anonymous sign-up features.

Nookal.description: Nookal is a practice management software that organizes and manages medical appointments.
Nookal.history: Nookal was founded in 2010 in Oxenford, Queensland, Australia by Jamie Chalmers and Darren Rieck as an allied health practice management platform. It has remained independent and serves clinics across Australia and internationally.
Nookal.summary_abuse: Nookal has no notable abuse signal. It is a medical practice management SaaS for allied health clinics with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Nootiz.description: Nootiz is a visual feedback tool designed to streamline the process of collecting and managing user feedback on websites.
Nootiz.history: Nootiz is a visual website feedback tool for collecting and managing client or team comments directly on web pages. No notable founding year or corporate history is publicly documented.
Nootiz.summary_abuse: Nootiz has no notable abuse signal. It is a feedback collection tool for web teams with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

NoPaperForms.description: NoPaperForms is a cloud-based platform that automates and manages student enrollment processes.
NoPaperForms.history: NoPaperForms was founded in 2017 in Gurugram, India by Naveen Goyal and Suraj Sapra as a cloud-based student enrollment and admissions automation platform. The company later converted to a public entity in preparation for an IPO.
NoPaperForms.summary_abuse: NoPaperForms has no notable abuse signal. It is an education-sector enrollment management SaaS with no consumer content-hosting or anonymous sign-up features that would attract fraudsters.

nopCommerce.description: nopCommerce is an open-source ecommerce solution based on Microsoft's ASP​.NET Core framework and MS SQL Server 2012 (or higher) backend database.
nopCommerce.history: nopCommerce was created by Andrei Mazulnitsyn and first released in 2008 as an open-source ASP.NET-based ecommerce platform. It is maintained by nopCommerce team and has grown to become one of the most popular .NET ecommerce platforms, widely used by small and medium businesses.
nopCommerce.summary_abuse: nopCommerce is a self-hosted open-source ecommerce platform. Like any ecommerce platform it can be installed and configured by fraudsters to create fake storefronts, but its self-hosted nature means responsibility lies with deployers. No systematic abuse of the platform distribution itself is documented.

nopStation.description: nopStation is a one stop ecommerce solution with custom integrations and custom built plugins based on custom tailored requirements on top of nopCommerce.

Notion.history: Notion was founded in 2016 by Ivan Zhao and Simon Last in San Francisco. By 2021 it reached a $10B valuation after raising $275M, becoming one of the most prominent productivity tools globally.
Notion.summary_abuse: Notion has low but non-zero abuse signal. Its public pages and database features have occasionally been used to host phishing content or fake login pages due to Notion's legitimate domain being trusted by URL filters. Notion actively responds to abuse reports and takes down malicious pages. The platform has published an AUP and takedown contact.

Notix.description: Notix is a web push notifications service for audience re-engagement.

Notix's free tier with easy sign-up increases risk exposure, though no specific documented incidents for this vendor are known.

NTLM.description: NTLM is an authentication method commonly used by Windows servers
NTLM.history: NTLM (NT LAN Manager) is a Microsoft proprietary authentication protocol first introduced in Windows NT in 1993. It succeeded the older LM protocol and was itself superseded by Kerberos as the preferred authentication method in Active Directory domains starting with Windows 2000. Microsoft has long advised migration away from NTLM due to known security weaknesses including pass-the-hash attacks and relay attacks, and in 2023 announced formal deprecation of NTLMv1.
NTLM.summary_abuse: NTLM has well-documented security vulnerabilities including pass-the-hash attacks, NTLM relay attacks, and credential theft. Attackers commonly exploit NTLM in internal network compromise scenarios to move laterally or elevate privileges. NTLMv1 in particular is considered insecure and was formally deprecated by Microsoft. NTLM relay attacks remain a common Active Directory exploitation technique. Despite this, NTLM is a protocol specification with no consumer-facing surface for scam or phishing hosting.

Nucleus CMS.description: Nucleus CMS is a free, open-source web application framework and content management system.
Nucleus CMS.history: Nucleus CMS was created by Belgian computer science student Wouter Demuynck, with version 1.0 released in 2002. Nudgify.summary_abuse: Nudgify is a social proof notification service. The product category is associated with dark patterns (false or misleading scarcity claims, fabricated activity notifications) that manipulate buyers.
